description_long: This is a compact, submachine gun-style rifle with a cylindrical main receiver and a prominent, detachable box magazine extending downward. The weapon features a tubular barrel with a simple front sight and a skeletal, vertical foregrip for improved handling. The receiver is mostly smooth and dark, likely constructed from metal, with a few visible mechanical details such as the charging handle and ejection port. The magazine is rectangular and appears to be made of metal, designed for rapid reloading. The rear of the weapon lacks a traditional stock, instead featuring a pistol grip for one-handed or close-quarters use. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functionality, making it suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, dark-colored submachine gun with a cylindrical receiver, detachable box magazine, and skeletal foregrip. It features a pistol grip and minimalistic design, optimized for close-quarters use.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the front, showing the narrow profile of the barrel and foregrip, with the magazine visible to the side.
description_view_1: A clear side view reveals the cylindrical receiver, skeletal foregrip, pistol grip, and the full length of the detachable box magazine.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the back of the receiver and the magazine, with the barrel and foregrip aligned in the background.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the same main features as view 1, with the magazine and foregrip visible, but from the other side.
